evals.name: who-can-reach
evals.prompt: Who is allowed to reach the cartservice on prod-cluster?
evals.expected_output: Runs `cub-netpol preflight` then `cub-netpol who-can-reach cartservice --cluster prod-cluster` and reports the source workloads. Read-only.

evals.name: reachable-from
evals.prompt: What can the frontend talk to in apptique on prod-cluster?
evals.expected_output: Runs `cub-netpol reachable-from frontend --cluster prod-cluster` (optionally --namespace apptique) and reports the destinations.

evals.name: is-A-allowed-to-reach-B
evals.prompt: Is checkoutservice allowed to reach paymentservice?
evals.expected_output: Runs `cub-netpol who-can-reach paymentservice` (or `reachable-from checkoutservice`) and checks whether checkoutservice is in the result, answering yes/no. Does not mutate.

evals.name: open-cluster-signal
evals.prompt: Why can everything reach everything in dev-cluster?
evals.expected_output: Explains that an open cluster (no NetworkPolicies) yields a full mesh, and notes this means a default-deny is missing — routing to netpol-audit/netpol-fix for remediation rather than treating it as a connectivity bug.
